# chronosphere_service_account (Resource)

A non-human identity used to authenticate API requests against Chronosphere. An API token is generated at creation and returned only once; store it securely, as a lost token requires recreating the service account.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_service_account" "unrestricted" {
  name         = "ci-deployer"
  unrestricted = true
}

resource "chronosphere_service_account" "restricted_read_only" {
  name = "metrics-reader"
  restriction {
    permission = "READ_ONLY"
    labels     = { "team" = "platform" }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the service account. Immutable after creation.

### Optional

- `restriction` (Block List, Max: 1) Restricts the service account to a specific permission and optional metric label scope. Exactly one of `unrestricted` or `restriction` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--restriction))
- `slug` (String) Stable identifier for the service account. Generated from `name` if omitted. Immutable after creation.
- `unrestricted` (Boolean) If true, grants the service account access to all Chronosphere APIs within the access controls defined by team membership. Exactly one of `unrestricted` or `restriction` must be set.

### Read-Only

- `email` (String) Read-only: synthetic email address assigned to the service account by the server.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) Read-only: API token generated for the service account. Returned only at creation time; store it securely. If lost, the service account must be recreated.

<a id="nestedblock--restriction"></a>
### Nested Schema for `restriction`

Required:

- `permission` (String) Permission level granted by this restriction (e.g. metric read/write).

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String) Optional label matchers further scoping the restriction to metrics whose labels match these key/value pairs.
